name: Lawfulness, fairness, and transparency of processing
description: Personal data must be processed lawfully, fairly, and in a transparent manner in relation to the data subject.

subcontrols.name: Legal basis for processing is identified and documented for each processing activity
subcontrols.description: At least one lawful basis under Art. 6 applies.
subcontrols.guidance: Examine records of processing. Verify legal basis documented per activity.

subcontrols.name: Where consent is the legal basis, conditions for valid consent are met including freely given, specific, informed, and unambiguous indication
subcontrols.guidance: Examine consent mechanisms. Verify opt-in, granularity, and withdrawal process.

subcontrols.name: Data subjects are informed about processing at or before the time of collection via a privacy notice
subcontrols.guidance: Examine privacy notices. Verify Art. 13/14 required information included.

subcontrols.name: Processing of special categories of personal data is prohibited unless a specific exception applies
subcontrols.guidance: Examine processing records for special categories. Verify Art. 9 exception documented.

name: Purpose limitation
description: Personal data must be collected for specified, explicit, and legitimate purposes and not further processed in an incompatible manner.

subcontrols.name: Purposes of processing are specified and documented before processing begins
subcontrols.guidance: Examine records of processing activities. Verify purposes specified before collection.

subcontrols.name: Further processing is assessed for compatibility with original purposes
subcontrols.guidance: Examine compatibility assessments. Verify Art. 6(4) criteria applied.

name: Data minimization
description: Personal data must be adequate, relevant, and limited to what is necessary in relation to the purposes for which they are processed.

subcontrols.name: Only personal data necessary for each specific purpose is collected
subcontrols.guidance: Examine data collection forms and systems. Verify no excessive data collected.

subcontrols.name: Periodic reviews ensure data held remains relevant and necessary
subcontrols.guidance: Examine data review schedules and records. Verify periodic minimization reviews.

name: Accuracy of personal data
description: Personal data must be accurate and, where necessary, kept up to date. Every reasonable step must be taken to ensure inaccurate data is erased or rectified without delay.

subcontrols.name: Processes exist to verify and maintain the accuracy of personal data
subcontrols.guidance: Examine data quality procedures. Verify accuracy checks in place.

subcontrols.name: Inaccurate personal data is corrected or erased without undue delay upon discovery or request
subcontrols.guidance: Examine rectification request logs. Verify timely corrections.

name: Storage limitation
description: Personal data must be kept in a form which permits identification of data subjects for no longer than is necessary for the purposes for which it is processed.

subcontrols.name: Retention periods are defined for each category of personal data
subcontrols.guidance: Examine retention policy. Verify periods defined per data category.

subcontrols.name: Personal data is erased or anonymized when retention period expires
subcontrols.guidance: Examine deletion procedures and logs. Verify data deleted on schedule.

name: Integrity and confidentiality (security of processing)
description: Personal data must be processed in a manner that ensures appropriate security, including protection against unauthorized or unlawful processing and against accidental loss, destruction, or damage.

subcontrols.name: Pseudonymization and encryption of personal data are implemented where appropriate
subcontrols.guidance: Examine encryption implementations. Verify pseudonymization where applicable.

subcontrols.name: The ability to ensure ongoing confidentiality, integrity, availability, and resilience of processing systems and services
subcontrols.guidance: Examine system security architecture. Verify CIA controls.

subcontrols.name: The ability to restore the availability and access to personal data in a timely manner in the event of a physical or technical incident
subcontrols.guidance: Examine backup and DR procedures. Verify recovery testing.

subcontrols.name: A process for regularly testing, assessing, and evaluating the effectiveness of technical and organizational measures for ensuring the security of the processing
subcontrols.guidance: Examine security testing records. Verify periodic assessments conducted.

name: Accountability
description: The controller shall be responsible for, and be able to demonstrate compliance with, the data protection principles.

subcontrols.name: The controller implements appropriate technical and organizational measures to ensure and demonstrate compliance
subcontrols.guidance: Examine compliance documentation. Verify policies, procedures, and records.

subcontrols.name: Data protection policies are implemented and documented
subcontrols.guidance: Examine data protection policy. Verify it covers all GDPR principles.

subcontrols.name: Records of processing activities are maintained as required
subcontrols.guidance: Examine ROPA. Verify all required fields populated.

name: Rights of the data subject — Access and portability
description: Data subjects have the right to obtain confirmation of processing, access their data, and receive it in a portable format.
category: Chapter III: Rights of the Data Subject

subcontrols.name: Data subjects can obtain confirmation of whether personal data concerning them is being processed
subcontrols.guidance: Examine SAR handling process. Verify confirmation provided within 1 month.

subcontrols.name: A copy of the personal data undergoing processing is provided free of charge in a commonly used electronic format
subcontrols.guidance: Examine SAR response records. Verify data provided in accessible format.

subcontrols.name: Data subjects can receive their personal data in a structured, commonly used, and machine-readable format and transmit it to another controller
subcontrols.guidance: Examine portability request handling. Verify machine-readable format provided.

name: Rights of the data subject — Erasure and restriction
description: Data subjects have the right to obtain erasure of personal data and restriction of processing under certain conditions.
category: Chapter III: Rights of the Data Subject

subcontrols.name: Data subjects can obtain erasure of personal data without undue delay where one of the specified grounds applies
subcontrols.guidance: Examine erasure request handling. Verify deletion within 1 month.

subcontrols.name: Where erasure requests apply to data made public, reasonable steps are taken to inform other controllers processing the data
subcontrols.guidance: Examine notification procedures. Verify downstream controllers informed.

subcontrols.name: Data subjects can obtain restriction of processing where accuracy is contested, processing is unlawful, or data is no longer needed but required for legal claims
subcontrols.guidance: Examine restriction request handling. Verify processing halted appropriately.

name: Right to object and automated decision-making
description: Data subjects have the right to object to processing and not to be subject to decisions based solely on automated processing which produce legal or similarly significant effects.
category: Chapter III: Rights of the Data Subject

subcontrols.name: Data subjects can object to processing based on legitimate interests or public interest, including profiling
subcontrols.guidance: Examine objection handling process. Verify processing ceases unless compelling grounds.

subcontrols.name: Where personal data is processed for direct marketing, data subjects can object at any time and processing ceases
subcontrols.guidance: Examine marketing consent/opt-out mechanisms. Verify immediate cessation.

subcontrols.name: Data subjects are not subject to decisions based solely on automated processing that produce legal or similarly significant effects, unless explicit consent, contractual necessity, or law authorizes it
subcontrols.guidance: Examine automated decision systems. Verify human oversight and right to contest.

name: Data protection by design and by default
description: The controller shall implement appropriate technical and organizational measures designed to implement data-protection principles in an effective manner and integrate necessary safeguards into the processing.

subcontrols.name: Data protection considerations are integrated into the design of processing operations and systems from the earliest stage
subcontrols.guidance: Examine SDLC procedures. Verify privacy impact considered at design stage.

subcontrols.name: By default, only personal data necessary for each specific purpose is processed with regard to amount, extent, storage period, and accessibility
subcontrols.guidance: Examine default system configurations. Verify minimal data processing by default.

name: Processor requirements
description: Processing by a processor shall be governed by a contract or legal act that sets out the subject-matter and duration of the processing, the nature and purpose, the type of personal data, and the categories of data subjects.

subcontrols.name: Processors are engaged only where sufficient guarantees of appropriate technical and organizational measures are provided
subcontrols.guidance: Examine vendor assessment records. Verify security guarantees evaluated.

subcontrols.name: Processing by a processor is governed by a written contract containing all required Art. 28(3) clauses
subcontrols.guidance: Examine DPAs. Verify all mandatory clauses included.

subcontrols.name: Sub-processors are engaged only with prior written authorization of the controller
subcontrols.guidance: Examine sub-processor lists and authorizations. Verify controller approval.

name: Records of processing activities
description: Each controller and processor shall maintain a record of processing activities under its responsibility.

subcontrols.name: Controller maintains records containing name and contact details, purposes, categories of data subjects and data, recipients, transfers, retention periods, and security measures
subcontrols.guidance: Examine ROPA. Verify all Art. 30(1) required fields populated.

subcontrols.name: Processor maintains records containing name and contact details of each controller, categories of processing, transfers, and security measures
subcontrols.guidance: Examine processor ROPA. Verify Art. 30(2) fields complete.

subcontrols.name: Records are made available to the supervisory authority on request
subcontrols.guidance: Verify records accessible and current for regulatory requests.

name: Data breach notification
description: Personal data breaches must be notified to the supervisory authority within 72 hours and to data subjects without undue delay where the breach is likely to result in a high risk to their rights and freedoms.

subcontrols.name: Personal data breaches are notified to the supervisory authority without undue delay and where feasible within 72 hours of becoming aware
subcontrols.guidance: Examine breach notification procedures and logs. Verify 72-hour timeline met.

subcontrols.name: Breach notification to authority includes nature of breach, categories and approximate number of data subjects and records affected, likely consequences, and measures taken
subcontrols.guidance: Examine breach notifications. Verify all Art. 33(3) information included.

subcontrols.name: When a breach is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the data subject is informed without undue delay
subcontrols.guidance: Examine subject notification procedures. Verify high-risk breaches communicated.

subcontrols.name: A record of all personal data breaches is maintained including facts, effects, and remedial action taken
subcontrols.guidance: Examine breach register. Verify completeness and remediation documentation.

name: Data protection impact assessment
description: Where processing is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller shall carry out a data protection impact assessment prior to the processing.

subcontrols.name: DPIAs are conducted for processing likely to result in high risk, including systematic evaluation/profiling, large-scale processing of special categories, and large-scale monitoring of public areas
subcontrols.guidance: Examine DPIA records. Verify DPIAs conducted for all Art. 35(3) scenarios.

subcontrols.name: DPIA contains a systematic description of processing operations, assessment of necessity and proportionality, assessment of risks, and measures to address risks
subcontrols.guidance: Examine DPIA documents. Verify all Art. 35(7) elements included.

subcontrols.name: Where DPIA indicates high residual risk, the supervisory authority is consulted prior to processing
subcontrols.guidance: Examine prior consultation records. Verify authority consulted where required.

name: Data Protection Officer
description: A Data Protection Officer must be designated where the core activities require regular and systematic monitoring of data subjects on a large scale, or consist of large-scale processing of special categories of data.

subcontrols.name: A DPO is designated where required and their contact details are published and communicated to the supervisory authority
subcontrols.guidance: Examine DPO appointment. Verify contact details published and authority notified.

subcontrols.name: The DPO is involved properly and in a timely manner in all issues relating to the protection of personal data
subcontrols.guidance: Examine DPO consultation records. Verify timely involvement in privacy decisions.

subcontrols.name: The DPO independently performs tasks including informing, advising, monitoring compliance, providing advice on DPIAs, and cooperating with the supervisory authority
subcontrols.guidance: Examine DPO activity records. Verify independent task performance.

name: International data transfers
description: Transfers of personal data to third countries or international organizations may only take place where adequate safeguards are in place.

subcontrols.name: Transfers to third countries occur only on the basis of an adequacy decision, appropriate safeguards, or a derogation under Art. 49
subcontrols.guidance: Examine transfer records. Verify legal basis for each transfer destination.

subcontrols.name: Where standard contractual clauses (SCCs) are used, supplementary measures are assessed and implemented where necessary to ensure adequate protection
subcontrols.guidance: Examine TIAs and supplementary measures. Verify adequacy of protections.

subcontrols.name: Transfers and their legal bases are documented in the records of processing activities
subcontrols.guidance: Examine ROPA transfer entries. Verify transfer destinations and mechanisms documented.
